    • 1.0.1 22/01/2024 Updated table and figure structure

Detection mode project: Tables and figures for publication

Author

Martina Brayley

Published

May 23, 2025

version 5 - Using data missing as a separate category in analysis

version 6 - swapping HRT variable for HRT subtypes variable

version 7 -

version 8 - 21/01/2025 - publication edits - converted violin plots into functions - this script has the conversion code

version 9 - 23/01/2025 - clean code - used function for violin plots

0.0.1 Changes agreed with Montse 14/01/2025

  • Violin plots - MD by riskfactors keep unadjusted but swap individual p-value for p-trend

  • create a table for MD by riskfactors and add adjusted for age at mammo reading

  • create a crosstab for SD registry & derived dmode (expl done in analysis script - create a nice table)

  • formatting regression tables - remove missing rows estimates, but keep descriptives

  • update flow chart (remove exclusions, add another level for other dmode categories, removed MD numbers)

1 Table and figure structure:

Main tables

Table 1 - general descriptives (population characteristics)

Table 2 - Risk factor descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results from default model ( adjusted for multiple exposures and selected covariates) + model additionally adjusted for mammo density (add mediation measure)

Main figures

Figures 1 to 3 - comprised figure of plots describing mamographic density in relation to risk factors (detail in ST 2)

Supplementary material

(ST = Supplementary Table)

ST 1 - TC descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results for TC only + full model RF adjusting for TC + fulll RFmodel adjusting for TC and density

ST 2 - difference in characteristics between those with and without mammographic density

1.0.1 22/01/2024 Updated table and figure structure

based on draft paper

Figure 1: Flow chart for selection of Generations Study breast cancer cases eligible for analyses (created in drawio (saved into the paper folder) and mermaid (memaid issue with formatting)

Figure 2: Mammographic density by detection mode (TBC)

Figure 3: Mammographic density across risk factor categories

Figure 4: Mammographic density across tumour characteristics

Table 1: Tumour characteristics of interval and screen-detected breast cancers

Table 1b: Tumour characteristics of interval and screen-detected breast cancers with stratification by mammographic density

Table 2: Risk factors of interval and screen-detected breast cancers

Table 3: Tumour characteristics and risk factors, by detection mode - multivar models* including a) all cases, b) cases with mammographic density data, c) adjustment for mammographic density

2 Main figures and tables

2.2 Descriptives of risk factors with MD

2.2.0.1 Data transformation - formal statistical test of differences between categories

29/07/2024 decided to use square root transformation as data not normally distributed. Linear regression is then used to and p-values (what about estimate?) will be then added to the figures.

2.2.0.2 Distribution before and after transformation (for info only)

Figure for internal info only - data approximately normally distributed with more symmetry (checked with Michael and agreed this is appropriate for linear regression)

Notes: full code with more formal tests and distribution descriptives is in general_descriptives.qmd file

Note

Missing categories left in for now for info but will probably need to be removed

2.2.1 Figure 2 - Mammographic density by detection mode

2.2.2 Figure 3 - Mammographic density by risk factors

Question

The layout to be decided - currently there are several composite plots by risk factor groups

2.2.2.1 Ordering for RF plots

(chronological order)

Composite plot 1

plot 7 - Age at menarche

plot 8 - Oral contraceptives

Composite plot 2

plot 5 - n of parous preg

plot 6 - age at first birth

plot 15 - Breastfeeding duration

Composite plot 3

plot 2 - Meno status

plot 3 - age meno

plot 4 - HRT

Composite plot 4

plot 9 - BBD

plot 10 - Family history of breast cancer

Composite plot 5

plot 16 - BMI age 20

plot 13 - BMI entry

plot 14 - physical activity

Composite plot 6

plot 11 - Alcohol

plot 12 - Smoking status

# All plots combined
plot <- plot_grid(plotlist = list(agemenarche_plot, oc_plot, parity_plot, agebirth1_plot, bf_plot, menost_plot, menoage_plot, hrtst_plot, bbd_plot, fambrca_plot, bmi20_plot, bmi_plot, pa_plot, alc_plot, smok_plot),
          nrow = 5)

plot
ggsave(plot, filename = "../outputs/figures/main/figure_3.png", height = 20, width = 15)

Note

There is some bug with labelling the variables as the table is a composite of two types of tables. I tried a few different ways but needs further reviewing.

2.3 Table 2 - Main analysis

The main analysis is a logistic regression with multiple exposures (risk factors).

This table shows risk factor level descriptives and their associations with the mode of detection (logistic regression) adjusted for all other risk factors, age at diagnosis and time from entry to BC diagnosis.

Model 2 is the final selected model (Default model) to present in the analysis. Model 2 is adjusted for all co-exposures + age at diagnosis and time from entry to BC diagnosis. Model 1 was only adjusted for co-exposures, e.i. all exposures in the model.

Because model 2 was selected as the default model, it is also used on the subanalyses with TC and MD as the comparison against adjusted models for TC and MD.

03/10/2024 - adding breastfeeding and BMI age 20 to the models, changed OC status to never/ever

Model 1: “d_R1alcohol_status_cat”, “d_R1alcohol_units_cat”, “d_R1smokingstatus_cat”, “d_bmi_entry_cat”, “d_R1physmet_leis_who”, “d_bbd”, “d_fambrca”, “d_age_menarche_cat”, “d_ocstatus_cat”, “d_R1menopause_cat3”, “d_age_menopause_cat”, “d_R1hrtstatus_cat”, “d_parous_cat”, “d_parity_cat”, “d_agebirth1_cat” + breastfeeding duration + bmi at 20

Model 2: Model 1 + “diagage”, “d_R1toBC_y”, “yeardiag”

Model 3: Model 2 + “d_md_qrt”

Model 4: Model 3 + d_MDtoBC_lab (categorical)

2.3.2 Supplementary Table 3

on 01/10/2024 agreed to:

  • remove percentages

  • use HRT simple variable (never/former/current)

  • Use OC status simple variable (ever/never)

  • remove p for categories

  • add overall p-value (only for model 4?)

should Forest plot also be presented?

29/01/2025

Do we still want the forest plot figures?

png 
  2

Note:

Some edits in the forest plot are still needed

- ordering on the forest plot to be fixed - each row in the plot is also a row in the data behind the plot so not easily sorted by variable

- BMI categories also jumped but reference is normal weight

- scale even when specific ticks selected it still defaults to ticks by 0.5 points

n (%); Mean (SD)

3.3 Cross-tabulation between registry detection mode and in-house detection mode

#| echo: false

# Read in data
df_mod <- readRDS("Q:/SHARED/USERS/MBrayley/Screening/data/dm_df_include_unknown.rds")

# use row percentages and rename reg_sd levels to align with dmode

dmode_crosstab_tb <- df_mod |>
  filter(source_dm2 != "reg_sd") |>
  mutate(
    
    reg_sd = if_else(is.na(reg_sd), "NA", reg_sd),
    
    reg_sd = factor(reg_sd, 
                    levels = c("N", "Y", "U", "NA"), 
                    labels = c("Interval", "Screen-Detected", "Unknown", "No Data")),
    
    d_dmode = case_when(
      ancat_dmode_v2 == "I" ~ "Interval",
      ancat_dmode_v2 == "SD" ~ "Screen-Detected",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    
    d_dmode = factor(d_dmode, levels = c("Interval", "Screen-Detected", "Other"))
    
  ) |> 
  tbl_cross(
    row = reg_sd,
    col = d_dmode,
    percent = "cell"
  ) |> 
  bold_labels()

#dmode_crosstab_tb |> show_header_names()


dmode_crosstab_tb <- dmode_crosstab_tb |> 
  modify_header(
    label ~ "**Registry Detection Mode**"
  ) |> 
  modify_spanning_header(all_stat_cols() ~ "**Derived Detection Mode**" # Rename column variable
  ) 


dmode_crosstab_tb <- dmode_crosstab_tb |> 
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x |> 
      filter(label != "reg_sd") |>  # Remove rows where the label matches the old variable name
      mutate(stat_1 = if_else(row_number() == n(), stat_1, gsub("\\(.*", "", stat_1)),
             stat_2 = if_else(row_number() == n(), stat_2, gsub("\\(.*", "", stat_2)),
             stat_3 = if_else(row_number() == n(), stat_3, gsub("\\(.*", "", stat_3)),
             )
  )
